relations.note: Geometry gives exact placement for the structural box/badge.
Radius spec refines the box/badge shape.
Fallback addresses client support for the absolute-positioning geometry.

quotes: Light Blue `#E6F0F9` fill, single accent shape
Brand Blue `#00529b`, Nunito Sans weight 800, ~21px / 28px line-height, left-aligned, **vertically centered** to the box
a **White `#ffffff` rounded square** holding the line icon (Brand Blue stroke)
A **light-blue accent-shape box** containing a short blue headline, with a **white icon badge that straddles the box's left edge**
Used for supporting "proof point" statements (dosing convenience, time-to-effect, etc.).
Never let copy slide under the badge.

rules.intent: Preserve the signature icon-callout proof-point device.
rules.rule_text: The Icon Callout Box is a light-blue accent-shape box (icon_callout.box.fill = #E6F0F9) on a white section background (#ffffff), containing a short Brand Blue (#00529b) headline in Nunito Sans weight 800 (~21px / 28px line-height), left-aligned and vertically centered to the box. A white (#ffffff) icon badge holding a Brand Blue line icon straddles the box's left edge (half-on/half-off). It is used for supporting 'proof point' statements (dosing convenience, time-to-effect, etc.). Copy must never slide under the badge.

quotes: Let `W` = badge width (use **80px**, icon ?48px inside).
Inset the blue box from the left by **`W/2` = 40px**, and place the badge so its **center lands exactly on the box's left edge**
Vertical center via `top:50%` + `margin-top:?40px` (half the badge height).
Distance from badge center to box edge must be **`0`**.
**inset = `W/2`**, **box left-padding = `W/2 + 24px`**, **badge `margin-top` = `?H/2`**

rules.intent: Guarantee the exact 50/50 badge-on-edge alignment at any scale.
rules.rule_text: Badge geometry (exact): badge width W = 80px with a ~48px inner icon. Inset the blue box from the left by W/2 = 40px and place the badge so its center lands exactly on the box's left edge (distance from badge center to box edge must be 0). Vertically center the badge via top:50% + margin-top:-40px (half the badge height). The box left padding must clear the overhanging badge half plus a gap (≈ W/2 + 24px). If the badge size changes, keep the relationships: inset = W/2, box left-padding = W/2 + 24px, badge margin-top = -H/2.

quotes: box `border-radius: 40px 8px 40px 8px;` in `TL TR BR BL` order
`border-radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px;` (also 5:1, large TL+BR)
**Corner radius (mandatory, never change):**
Never simplify either to a single uniform radius, swap the corners, or flip the diagonal.

rules.intent: Lock the signature asymmetric 5:1 accent-shape radii.
rules.rule_text: Corner radius (mandatory, never change): box border-radius 40px 8px 40px 8px in TL TR BR BL order — large on top-left + bottom-right, near-point on top-right + bottom-left, a 5:1 ratio. The white badge mirrors the same accent shape at smaller scale, border-radius 20px 4px 20px 4px (also 5:1, large TL+BR). Never simplify either to a single uniform radius, swap the corners, or flip the diagonal.

quotes: `box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18);` (Dark Navy at low opacity)
the white icon badge **must carry a subtle drop shadow**
This shadow is required on every instance

rules.intent: Ensure the badge visibly lifts off the box on every instance.
rules.rule_text: Drop shadow (mandatory): the white icon badge must carry a subtle drop shadow (box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18), Dark Navy #011E45 at low opacity) so it lifts off the blue box. This shadow is required on every instance; without it the badge looks flat and pasted-on.

quotes: **Outlook (desktop, Word engine) ignores**
the brand-safe option is to **pre-composite the white badge (with its shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG**
every other rule (Light Blue fill, `40px 8px 40px 8px` radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow) stays identical

rules.intent: Preserve cross-client fidelity while allowing a graceful Outlook degrade.
rules.rule_text: The 50/50 overhang relies on position:absolute, which Apple Mail / iOS / most web clients honor but Outlook (desktop, Word engine) ignores — there the badge drops to top-left and the box won't be inset. For pixel-perfect fidelity across all clients, the brand-safe option is to pre-composite the white badge (with its shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG placed via the same absolute positioning, or bake the entire badge-on-edge treatment into the box's background image while keeping the text live. When that's not feasible, ship the live version and accept the Outlook degrade (badge flush at the inner-left edge); every other rule (Light Blue fill, 40px 8px 40px 8px radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow) stays identical.

quotes: <div style="position:absolute; left:0; top:50%; margin-top:-40px; width:80px; height:80px; background-color:#ffffff; border-radius:20px 4px 20px 4px; box-shadow:0 4px 10px rgba(1,30,69,0.18); text-align:center; line-height:80px;">
